title: Automatically move elements between tables within the next 7 days

posts.author: Gabriel1 (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am facing the following problem. I have created two tables, one is for the monthly view, the other only contains events for the next 7 days. First, I enter all events in the monthly view. Now I want the events that take place in the next 7 days to be displayed in the other table instead.

I tried it with automations, but it didn’t work or I haven’t found the right one yet. Can someone please help me?

Thank you very much!

monday1385×898 119 KB

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello! It sounds like you’re trying to automate the transfer of events from your monthly view to your 7-day view. Please ensure that your automation is set to trigger based on the date of the events. You might want to explore the automation recipes available in Monday.com to find one that fits your needs. If you need further assistance, feel free to schedule a call with us at Thewild6.com.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, It sounds like you’re on the right track with automations! You can achieve this by setting up an automation that automatically moves or mirrors events from your monthly view table to your 7-day events table based on the event date.

Try the following automation setup:

  1. Trigger: “When a date arrives” → Set it to trigger when the event date is within the next 7 days.
  2. Action: “Move item to board” → Select your 7-day events table as the destination.

Alternatively, if you want the events to stay in the monthly view but also appear in the 7-day view, you can use a mirrored column and a filtered view instead of moving them.

Let me know if you need further guidance!

Best,

posts.author: Gabriel1 (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, thank you for your support:)

It didn’t work, can you please help me?
